channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IMD warns of continued heatwave over central India until end of May
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a warning that severe heatwave conditions are likely to continue over central India till the end of May 2026. According to the latest advisory, reg...

item.title: 1944 Volkswagen Type 82 Kübelwagen: Design and Role of Germany’s WWII Utility Vehicle
item.description: The 1944 Volkswagen Type 82 Kübelwagen was a key light utility vehicle used by German forces during World War II. Designed by Ferdinand Porsche, it was developed from the early Volkswagen Beetle platf...

item.title: Supreme Court schedules 2026 Shari’ah Special Bar Examinations on May 24 and 27 across four testing centres
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has officially announced the schedule for the 2026 Shari’ah Special Bar Examinations (SSBE), which will be conducted on May 24 and May 27. These examinations are i...

item.title: High-income investors may shift from property to sharemarket gearing as tax rules tighten
item.description: The article examines how recent changes in Australia’s federal budget are reshaping investment strategies among high-income earners, particularly in relation to negative gearing. Under the new measure...

item.title: How Kenya can learn from the UK approach to reducing knife-related violence
item.description: Knife-related violence is increasingly becoming a serious concern in Kenya, especially in urban centres, informal settlements, and during political events where stabbings, muggings, and armed robberie...

item.title: Baller Room founders reframe Pinoy basketball culture through art, music, and community spaces
item.description: The article features artists Charles Salazar (also known as Tsarlyboy) and Finney Santos, founders of the Baller Room, a creative project that blends live basketball games with DJ sets and visual art....

item.title: Benue Police Arrest Four Suspects Involved in Kidnapping and Human Trafficking
item.description: The Police Command for Benue State don arrest four suspects wey dem link to kidnapping and human trafficking as dem dey intensify operations against violent crimes for the state. According to Police S...

item.title: Hospital payment policies had greater impact on U.S. female sterilization rates than 1970s consent reforms
item.description: A new study by a health economist and a political scientist examines long-term shifts in female sterilization trends in the United States and the policy forces that shaped them. The researchers compar...

item.title: Morné Steyn praises Chris Smith’s form and questions Springbok omission despite standout URC season
item.description: Former Springbok fly-half Morné Steyn has publicly backed Lions playmaker Chris Smith as the standout No. 10 in South African rugby at present, expressing surprise that the 30-year-old was not include...

item.title: Police Chief Urges Citizens to Avoid Mob Justice and Follow Legal Procedures
item.description: Tanzania’s Inspector General of Police, Camillus Wambura, has strongly warned citizens across the country against engaging in mob justice or taking the law into their own hands. He emphasized that all...

item.title: Photos of Everyday American Family Life in the 1950s from the David Wipf Collection
item.description: The 1950s marked a significant period in American history characterized by economic prosperity, suburban expansion, and a strong emphasis on family values following the end of World War II. This era e...

item.title: Edo Police Rescue All Remaining Six Kidnapped Passengers on Benin-Lagos Highway
item.description: The Edo State Police Command has confirmed the successful rescue of the remaining six passengers who were kidnapped during a violent attack along the Benin–Lagos Road. The victims were part of a group...

item.title: US changes green card rules requiring applicants to apply from outside the country
item.description: The US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) has announced a major policy change that will require most foreign nationals already in the United States to leave the country and apply for permane...

item.title: Saba offers a rare, unspoiled Caribbean experience focused on nature, hiking and local culture
item.description: Saba, a small Dutch Caribbean island of just 13 square kilometres and about 2,100 residents, is presented as one of the region’s most distinctive and least commercialised destinations. Often called th...

item.title: NBDSA pulls up Zee News over unverified broadcasts linked to Muslims and communal claims
item.description: The News Broadcasting and Digital Standards Authority (NBDSA) has censured Zee News for airing allegedly misleading and communally sensitive programmes that relied on unverified claims and speculation...

item.title: Tanzania enforces restrictions on foreign participation in locally reserved business sectors
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has stepped up enforcement measures aimed at protecting business sectors that are officially reserved for Tanzanian citizens. The move is part of a wider strategy to ensure ...

item.title: GMA News ranks 3rd globally in Tubular Labs April 2026 digital video news rankings
item.description: GMA News has achieved a major milestone in global digital media performance after ranking No. 3 worldwide in the Tubular Labs Leadership Rankings for News and Politics for April 2026. The Philippine n...

item.title: South African education department warns public about fake ‘Teacher Assistant Phase 6’ job advertisements circulating on Facebook
item.description: South Africa’s Department of Basic Education has issued a warning to the public about fraudulent social media posts advertising supposed ‘Teacher Assistants Phase 6’ job opportunities for 2026. These ...

item.title: How a Canadian province could legally pursue separation under the Clarity Act
item.description: The article explains the legal framework governing whether a Canadian province can unilaterally secede from the country. It highlights that, according to a 1998 Supreme Court of Canada reference case ...

item.title: Tanzania outlines measures to manage fuel price increases amid global oil market tensions
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced a series of measures aimed at reducing the impact of rising fuel prices caused by ongoing tensions in the Gulf region and disruptions in global oil markets. Th...
